title: Carvana Tops 1 Million Sales: No Showrooms, But Lot Of Bricks And Mortar Behind The Scenes
text: Carvana Co., the e-commerce platform for buying and selling used cars, passed a milestone in Q4, having sold more than 1 million vehicles in its roughly nine-year history.

title: Carvana Says Purchase of Auction Company, Facilities Will Expand Next-Day Delivery Capability
text: Next-day delivery will be available to more of the customers who buy a used car from automotive retailer Carvana. With its newly announced acquisition of the nation's second-largest used vehicle auction company and its 56 sites in the U.S., Carvana will have facilities within 50 miles of 58% of the country's population — up from […]

title: Russian Troops Advance on Kyiv as Talks in Belarus Are on the Table
text: U.S. stocks are gaining, along with those in Europe, as officials in Russia and Ukraine held out the possibility of negotiations in Belarus.

title: Carvana (CVNA) Q4 Earnings Miss Estimates, Sales Rise Y/Y
text: Carvana (CVNA) incurs a loss in the fourth quarter of 2021, but sales rise year over year. The company provides guidance for 2022 amidst an anticipated tough year ahead.

title: Carvana to buy KAR Global's U.S. vehicle auction business for over $2 billion
text: Online used-car seller Carvana has agreed to buy KAR Global's vehicle auction business in the United States for $2.2 billion to boost its physical presence and maintain growth as a pandemic-induced boom fades.

title: Carvana posts wider Q4 loss, says that rising used-car prices dent business
text: Carvana Co. CVNA, +10.91% stock fell nearly 6% in the extended session Thursday after the online used-car retailer posted a wider-than-expected quarterly loss and said that high used-car prices are causing potential buyers to give up or delay buying a vehicle. Carvana said it lost $182 million, or $1.02 a share, in the fourth quarter, compared with a loss of $154 million, or 87 cents a share, in the year-ago quarter.

title: Carvana Inks Strategic Partnership With ForeverCar
text: Carvana Co (NYSE: CVNA) has signed a partnership agreement with the digital omnichannel vehicle service contract platform, ForeverCar. ForeverCar is part of CUNA Mutual Group, Madison, Wisconsin.

title: Why Carvana Stock Tanked 30.1% in January
text: Forward expectations for the used car market and the broad market sell-off hurt Carvana stock last month.

title: Carvana Is Looking Attractive, But Wait For Some More Time
text: The share price is down more than 60% from its 52-week high. Carvana sold 111,949 cars in Q3 2021, which were 74% higher year-over-year.
